description_long: This object consists of two upright stone grave markers set on a rectangular patch of ground covered with fallen leaves and some green vegetation. The larger marker is a tall, rectangular monolith with a weathered surface and a squared-off top, featuring a shallow rectangular recess on one face. The smaller marker is shorter and more irregular in shape, with a rough, aged texture and a rounded top. Both stones exhibit signs of moss and lichen growth, indicating long-term outdoor exposure. The ground beneath is uneven, with a mix of brown leaves and patches of green, suggesting a natural, minimally maintained graveyard setting. No visible inscriptions or decorative elements are present on either marker.
description: Two weathered stone grave markers, one tall and rectangular with a recessed panel, and one shorter and rougher, set on a leafy patch of ground.
description_view_0: Front view showing both grave markers, with the taller stone in the foreground and the shorter one behind, both standing on a leafy ground.
description_view_1: View highlighting the recessed rectangular panel on the taller marker, with the shorter marker partially obscured behind.
description_view_2: Rear view of the markers, showing the plain backs and the mossy, weathered surfaces of both stones.
description_view_3: Angled view displaying the recessed panel on the taller marker and the full profile of the shorter, rounded stone.
